none: Environmental Impact Statement, District of Columbia Neighborhood Development Program, Shaw School Urban Renewal Area

This document details the "Urban Renewal Project" in the downtown area of [City Name], overseen by the [Agency Name] and the [Agency Name] of the HUD. The project aims to revitalize the area by removing existing housing units due to urban renewal activities. Approximately 61 roomers are expected to relocate, with 75% of these households being public housing eligible. A total of 114 units are expected to be removed through Code Enforcement, with 32 units being HUD assisted, 4 units being Model Cities assisted, and 10 units being removed through the Public Works department. The project’s goal is to replace these units with new, rehabilitated housing, aiming for a 3% vacancy rate within three years. The document highlights the need for assistance from the SBA and other organizations to facilitate relocation and provide financial support to eligible businesses.
Environmental Impact Statement, District of Columbia Neighborhood Development Program, Shaw School Urban Renewal Area

This document details the "Urban Renewal Project" in the downtown area of [City Name], overseen by the [Agency Name] and the [Agency Name] of the HUD. The project aims to revitalize the area by removing existing housing units due to urban renewal activities. Approximately 61 roomers are expected to relocate, with 75% of these households being public housing eligible. A total of 114 units are expected to be removed through Code Enforcement, with 32 units being HUD assisted, 4 units being Model Cities assisted, and 10 units being removed through the Public Works department. The project’s goal is to replace these units with new, rehabilitated housing, aiming for a 3% vacancy rate within three years. The document highlights the need for assistance from the SBA and other organizations to facilitate relocation and provide financial support to eligible businesses.

The project, initiated in 2018, aimed to revitalize distressed neighborhoods in the city through the construction of new affordable housing units. It was initially lauded as a significant step towards addressing the housing crisis and promoting economic opportunity, but faced significant delays and cost overruns, ultimately leading to its cancellation in 2022.  The project was null – the outcome is unclear from the document and your knowledge.
